title: Talking about yourself
desc: Share your life with a new friend
setting: At a gathering, someone you just met is curious about you and starts asking questions.

steps.speaker: Karla
steps.en: And you, what do you do for work?

options.en: I'm a programmer. I work with computers.
options.feedback: Clear and complete. 'Soy' + profession is exactly right.
That dodges the question! She wants to know what you do.
Understandable, just a little terse. A full sentence feels warmer.

steps.speaker: Karla
steps.en: How interesting! And do you have family here?

options.es: No, mi familia vive en Estados Unidos.
options.en: No, my family lives in the US.
options.feedback: Natural and honest. 'Vive en' tells her where they are.

options.es: Tengo dos hermanos, pero viven lejos.
options.en: I have two siblings, but they live far away.
options.feedback: Lovely detail — sharing a little extra invites connection.
A 'yes' with no detail closes the door. Give her something to follow up on.

steps.speaker: Karla
steps.en: And do you feel at home here in Nicaragua?

options.es: Me encanta. La gente es muy cálida.
options.en: I love it. The people are very warm.
options.feedback: A warm answer that compliments her people — that lands beautifully.
Honest, but a bit flat. If you do like it, let it show!

options.es: Sí me hallo, gracias a la gente.
options.en: Yes I feel at home, thanks to the people.
options.feedback: Great — you even reused her word 'hallar.' Sounds very natural.

steps.speaker: Karla
steps.es: Pues ya sos uno más de nosotros.
steps.en: Well, you're one of us now.

options.es: Eso me hace muy feliz. Gracias.
options.en: That makes me very happy. Thanks.
options.feedback: Heartfelt and natural. A perfect response to being welcomed in.
'Igualmente' doesn't quite fit here — there's nothing to return. A simple thanks is better.
Pleasant, but a warmer reply would honor the kind thing she said.
